candidates.evidence_quote: We have experienced significant disruptions to our business as federal, state and local restrictions have fluctuated over time to mitigate the spread of the COVID-19 virus
we incurred increased restaurant staff absenteeism and temporary shifts in consumer behavior
temporary shifts in consumer behavior, such as changes in customer traffic or the mix between on-premise and off-premise channels
we experienced labor shortfalls relative to our sales levels in certain parts of our workforce and/or geographies
we also began to experience certain supply shortages and transportation delays largely attributable to impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic
contributed to significantly increased commodity and wage inflation and other increased costs
contributed to significantly increased commodity and wage inflation and other increased costs
We have also encountered delays in opening new restaurants due to supply chain challenges, as well as to longer lead times in obtaining licenses and permits
we may again implement mitigation actions such as suspending share repurchases, not declaring future dividends, increasing borrowings under our credit facility
we may again implement mitigation actions such as suspending share repurchases, not declaring future dividends, increasing borrowings under our credit facility
we may again implement mitigation actions such as suspending share repurchases, not declaring future dividends, increasing borrowings under our credit facility
alcoholic beverage sales represented 12% of The Cheesecake Factory restaurant sales
for off-premise consumption, sales of which comprised approximately 25% of The Cheesecake Factory restaurant sales during fiscal 2022
results in a significant level of dessert sales, representing approximately 17% of The Cheesecake Factory sales during fiscal 2022
We have historically targeted menu price increases of approximately 2% to 3% annually, utilizing a market-based strategy to help mitigate cost pressure in higher-wage geographies
we implemented price increases above our historical levels to support our longer-term restaurant-level margin objectives
We will continue to consider the cost environment and consumer price sensitivity when evaluating future menu price increases
The average check for each customer, including beverages and desserts, was approximately $29.40 during fiscal 2022

Average sales per location for The Cheesecake Factory restaurants open for the full year were approximately $12.1 million and $11.8 million on a 53-week and 52-week basis, respectively, for fiscal 2022

Average sales per productive square foot (defined as all interior square footage plus seasonally adjusted exterior patio square footage) for restaurants open for the full year were approximately $1,116 and $1,089
Fluctuations in both average sales per location and average sales per productive square foot generally track with comparable restaurant sales trends
Total construction costs to build our restaurant premises average approximately $1,000 per interior square foot
Restaurant-level preopening costs for a typical location in an established market average approximately $1.0 million to $1.5 million
allow for additional customer capacity at a comparatively low occupancy cost per seat
we receive initial development fees, site and design fees and ongoing royalties based on our licensees’ restaurant sales
we leverage opportunities in the consumer packaged goods channel by partnering with third-party manufacturers
utilize our bakery production capacity by selling cheesecakes and other baked products to external foodservice operators, retailers and distributors
Average sales per mature location, defined as open prior to 2020 and adjusted to exclude impaired locations, for North Italia restaurants were approximately $8.0 million for fiscal 2022
One of our competitive strengths is our ability to anticipate customer preferences and adapt our expansive menu to the latest trends
due to actual-to-theoretical food cost inefficiencies and labor productivity inefficiencies commonly associated with new, highly complex restaurants
due to actual-to-theoretical food cost inefficiencies and labor productivity inefficiencies commonly associated with new, highly complex restaurants
our people practices contribute significantly to our ability to attract talent and to The Cheesecake Factory restaurants’ historically industry-leading retention rates

notes: Chunk contains a single event: part 1 of 6 of the FY2022 10-K (Item 1 Business). No fiscal_kpis in this chunk (present only on chunk_001).
All 32 evidence quotes verified as exact substrings of the event content.

Skipped (8): geopolitical unrest (too broad/vague as a standalone cause here); Fortune 100 / culture awards (no causal market variable); DEI program detail (not a price driver); FRC/Flower Child unit growth targets (banned word 'growth', folded into new_restaurant_development); Western Europe licensing expansion (folded into international_licensing_royalties); license/permit lead times (folded into new_restaurant_development); third-party delivery service (folded into off_premise_sales_mix); new-restaurant 'honeymoon' sales pattern (instance-bound maturation pattern, not a reusable cause).
share_repurchases/dividend_payments/credit_facility_borrowings share one quote per R2 (multiple independent causal variables in one sentence).
